slides.heading: Flat Vowels
slides.content: Japanese vowels (a, i, u, e, o) are always short and consistent. They never change sound.

slides.heading: Double Consonants
slides.content: A small 'tsu' (っ) creates a tiny pause. It's like a 'hiccup' in the middle of a word.

slides.heading: Long Vowels
slides.content: Sometimes vowels are held longer. 'Obasan' (Aunt) vs 'Obaasan' (Grandmother) is a big difference!

slides.heading: Context Clues
slides.content: If you don't catch a word, listen for the particles like 'wa' or 'ka' to understand the sentence goal.

slides.heading: Natural Speed
slides.content: Native speakers often blend sounds. Don't worry if it sounds fast; your ears will adapt!
